title: carex × muelleriana
intro: Carex × muelleriana, also known as golden variegated sedge, is a type of ornamental grass native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive golden-yellow striped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as wounds, burns, and skin irritations. Research has also shown that Carex × muelleriana has various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can contribute to overall well-being and immunity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further studies are needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
